mappings.rationaleAI: The Manhattan Project developed the first atomic weapons through nuclear fission (A.5.a), with the Trinity test and bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki demonstrating blast, heat, and radioactive fallout effects (A.5.b).

Traces nuclear physics from the discovery of the atomic nucleus through to nuclear weapons, explicitly connecting fission science (A.5.a) to twentieth-century military technology and the dual-use nature of nuclear knowledge.

Covers nuclear fusion as exploited in thermonuclear weapons (hydrogen bombs), directly addressing fusion as a nuclear weapon energy source (A.5.a) alongside the physics of combining atomic nuclei to release energy.

Chadwick's discovery of the electrically neutral neutron enabled nuclear fission chain reactions and the development of atomic weapons (A.5); neutron bombardment is the fundamental mechanism of fission-based nuclear warheads (A.5.a).

Directly examines how nuclear weapons, Cold War deterrence (A.5, A.5.b), and billion-dollar military technology including self-propelled missiles (A.4) have transformed the nature of armed conflict from Clausewitz's classical framework to modern asymmetric combat.

The 1453 siege featured Mehmet II's advanced cannon technology (A.2.c) breaching Constantinople's masonry fortifications (B.2.b), illustrating the gunpowder-era transition in permanent fortification design (B.2.c).

Iron smelting replacing bronze as the dominant metal for weapons directly addresses construction materials (A.1.a), producing harder, more versatile shock weapons like swords and axes (A.1.b) and missile weapons like spearheads (A.1.c) accessible to broader populations.
Archimedes designed catapults and ship-tilting defensive devices for Syracuse's defense against Roman siege, exemplifying mechanical weapons (A.1.d) and tactical military engineering support (D.1).

The Great Wall is a monumental permanent fortification before gunpowder (B.2.b), constructed of rammed earth and later brick and stone as imperial defense against nomadic incursions, representing strategic engineering support on a massive scale (D.2).

Depicts HMS Temeraire, a sail-powered ship of the line distinguished at Trafalgar (C.2.b), being towed by a steam tug, symbolizing the technological transition from sail-powered to steam-powered naval warships (C.2.c).

Radio technology transformed military strategy, communication, and navigation (F.1), developing from Maxwell's electromagnetic theory through Marconi's wireless telegraphy, with direct applications in military command, detection, and coordination systems.

Maxwell's electromagnetic equations predicted electromagnetic waves and provided the theoretical foundation for radio and radar (F.1), technologies essential to military communication, navigation, warning, detection, and weapon guidance systems.

mappings.episodeTitle: The Charge of the Light Brigade
mappings.rationaleAI: The 1854 cavalry charge at Balaclava illustrates horses as animal mounts serving as weapon platforms (C.1.a) in mid-nineteenth-century warfare, and the vulnerability of mounted cavalry against entrenched artillery positions.

The Bronze Age collapse involves the military technologies of the era including bronze construction materials for weapons (A.1.a) and the disruption caused by the Sea Peoples' invasions using shock weapons (A.1.b).
